Notice And Motion To Compel Depositions And For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Continued To Feb-28-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS EDUARDO MARQUEZ, LIDIA RUELAS' Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions.

This motion is the companion to Plaintiff's motion to compel the depositions of Marquez and Ruelas discussed above. Defendants seek a protective order to prevent the deposition of Marquez and Ruelas until Arturo Marquez either appears, is dismissed or is defaulted from the case. For the reasons discussed above, Defendants' motion for a protective order is denied. Defendants' request for sanctions is denied.

Plaintiff's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Tim Nardell at tim@ncalegal.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tim Nardell; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-15-2024 Continued To Feb-28-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice And Motion To Compel Depositions And For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys
Off Calendar Feb-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS FRANCISCO GARCIA, WALTER OCHOA, RAUL ZAPPELLA'S Motion To Compel Depositions And For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys is granted.

The parties have filed cross-motions regarding the taking of depositions of Defendants Eduardo Marquez and Lidia Ruelas. After correspondence between the counsel for the parties, Plaintiffs noticed the depositions for February 22 and 23, 2024. Plaintiffs take the position that these depositions should proceed, and have filed a motion to compel. Defendants take the position that they should not be required make Marquez and Ruelas available for deposition until a Doe Defendant (Arturo Marquez) either appears or is defaulted in the action. They argue that it would be unduly burdensome to produce Marquez and Ruelas for deposition now, when their depositions could be compelled a second time after Arturo Marquez appears in the action.

It appears to the Court that the risk of multiple depositions being required from Marquez and Ruelas is speculative. Regardless of whether the Doe Defendant is served and appears or defaults in this action, discovery between the existing parties should proceed, with the trial date, already continued once, currently set for June 2024. While it may be logical to wait for all Defendants to appear in the action, in the absence of an agreement between the parties about the scheduling of discovery, one party cannot dictate the scheduling to the other party. Marquez and Ruelas's depositions appear to have been noticed properly. The depositions should occur at a mutually convenient date, within 15 days of this Order.

Defendants also argue that Plaintiffs failed to give sufficient notice of this motion, as required under Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). This objection appears to have merit. With the two court holidays occurring in February the motion appears to have been noticed without the required notice. However, Defendants managed to respond to the motion and even filed their own motion for protective ordered, for which the court issued an order to shorten time, so that both motions could be heard on the same date. As to the substance of the motion to compel, the Court finds that objection to the scheduling of the motion is waived.

Nonetheless, given the procedural irregularity and the justifications of Defendants to delay the depositions -- which were not illogical though also not meritorious -- the Court denies Plaintiff's request for sanctions.

Plaintiff's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Tim Nardell at tim@ncalegal.com.
